name: Golden Cash Exchange
description_short: San Antonio-based precious metals and luxury goods buyer specializing in gold, silver, platinum, diamonds, and high-end watches. Offers free appraisals and immediate cash payouts.
description_long:
Golden Cash Exchange operates as a specialized pawn and precious metals buyer based in San Antonio, Texas. The company focuses on acquiring high-value items including precious metals, jewelry, coins, gemstones, and luxury goods, with an emphasis on bullion and numismatic coins across multiple metal categories.

The company buys an extensive range of precious metals and related items: gold jewelry (including dental gold, scrap, and designer pieces), silver coins and bullion, platinum and palladium items, certified diamonds and colored stones (2 carats and above), and high-end watches from brands like Rolex, Omega, and Cartier. They also purchase luxury handbags, vintage collectibles, and firearms. All transactions result in immediate cash payment to customers.

Golden Cash Exchange distinguishes itself through its specialized focus on precious metals and coins rather than general pawn operations. The company provides free appraisals and estimates, references market pricing from KITCO (a major precious metals pricing service), and claims to buy "anything of high value" with no stated upper limit. Their inventory breadth spans certified NGC/PCGS graded coins, foreign currencies, bullion bars and rounds from major mints, and estate collections.

The business model is straightforward: customers bring items for appraisal, receive a cash offer, and complete the transaction immediately. However, as with all pawn operations, customers should understand they are selling items outright rather than obtaining loans—there is no buyback period or redemption option. The company's website provides detailed lists of accepted items but limited information about valuation methodology or pricing transparency.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Free appraisals and estimates at multiple locations
Immediate cash payment for all transactions
Specializes in precious metals with reference to KITCO market pricing
Accepts certified graded coins (NGC/PCGS) and estate collections
Buys rare and international coins including Gold Eagles, Silver Maples, and foreign sovereigns
Extends beyond metals to luxury watches (Rolex, Omega, Cartier) and collectibles
Multiple San Antonio locations for convenience
cons: No loan redemption option—this is a sale, not a collateral loan like traditional pawn shops
Website contains no pricing information, valuation methodology, or fee disclosure
Limited transparency on how offers are calculated or how competitive they are versus competitors
No information on whether they match competing offers or provide price matching
Vague upper-limit policy ('anything of high value') with no stated boundaries or examples
best_for: People with precious metals, coins, or bullion to sell for immediate cash
Collectors liquidating gold coins, silver bullion, or numismatic collections
Those selling luxury watches, designer jewelry, or high-end handbags for quick cash
Estate executors or heirs needing to liquidate valuable jewelry and collectibles quickly
diagnosis: Golden Cash Exchange is best for individuals and estates with precious metals, coins, luxury goods, or collectibles who need immediate cash and prefer a specialized buyer over a general pawn shop. The primary caveat is that this is an outright purchase transaction, not a pawn loan—customers do not retain ownership or have a redemption period, making it suitable only for those definitively selling items rather than seeking temporary collateral-based funding.
services: Gold coin and bullion buying (Eagles, Maples, Pandas, Krugerrands, international coins)
Silver coin and bullion purchasing (bars, rounds, Morgan dollars, pre-1964 US coins)
Platinum and palladium coin and bullion acquisition
Scrap and jewelry gold buying (including dental gold, damaged pieces)
Certified diamond and colored stone buying (2 carats and above, GIA/EGL certified)
High-end watch purchasing (Rolex, Omega, Cartier, running or non-running)
Vintage collectibles purchasing (comic books, vintage firearms, vintage toys)
Estate jewelry, coins, and collection liquidation
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
